reviews.review_description: I was really hoping to experience this novel, but once it starts, the red dot remains stuck in the center to chapter select. I hold the Gear VR to center it everytime and still no response. Anyway to fix it before I refund this?

developer_response.body: We are very sorry for this matter, but this is Oculus's fault . We do not support GearVR but Oculus automatically sold TOKYO CHRONOS on GearVR store. You should refund and we hope you delete your review since this is Oculus's fault.

reviews.review_description:
ｌｉｎｋｔｒ．ｅｅ／２５ｖｒ

Here's to get some money off if you want to buy this game (make sure to remove all spaces and replace "DOT" with the dot character).
No cross buy if you brought for a Oculus GO and upgraded to a Quest. Not fair.

Started playing it, and love it. I got to chapter 2, so hopefully it'll last me long. Animation is beautiful, characters are rememberable. I don't like visual novels, but this hooked me from the very start!

I really enjoyed this. Beautifully realised characters, genuinely sympathetic protagonists,  interesting locations and amazing plot development.

The visual novel took me around 15 hours to fully complete, which I felt was about right (and in line with the description). With regard to being short, I don’t agree with this, and I wonder if those reviews claiming the game to be short actually fully completed the story. 

I don’t want spoil the story for anyone so I won’t go into details - but there is a walkthrough on the web, which may be useful if you think it was too short.

For me this was pretty well perfect.

I was really hoping it had choices you can make to change the story alot but not really and the only video part was after chapter 1 it is a great story though mind its my first full vr immersive book oculus should have more of them like this

What an amazing VR visual novel. The story was what you hope for - equal to or greater than PC or console visual novels - and I massively enjoyed it. The one caveat is an important character is introduced halfway through, but that doesn’t detract from the novel. Character models were extremely impressive. One thing I love about VR is that characters can get right up next to you, making hugs feel intimate and scare scenes terrifying (the game has one or two). Besides that, it played great on my oculus go, music-wise it’s on-point, and even with my speed-clicking extreme reading speed I still took 10 hours to complete it. I very highly recommend this game and hope to see more VRVNs in the future.
It was shorter then I was hoping for how much money it cost but definitely enjoyed it while it lasted!

I’m in my 3rd round of this game. 
-Amazing experience to really feel each character’s emotions 
-Great stories and full of surprises
-Cool characters
-Autosave and manual save 
-Comfortable, no motion sickness
New way to enjoy cm. Hoping to see other title from the developer.

I have experienced many experimental VR games so far. This game is a highly recommended experience for everyone.

-Experiences unique to VR.
Dramatic rendition with sounds with an orientation that matches the scene and animations that are used effectively. Everything is designed for the experience. It's not flashy at all, but it's a great match for the experience.

-A system that supports the experience.
The most time spent with VR mystery visual novels is reading. The system of this game is devised for comfortable reading. Words, sentences and images are effectively placed.

-Great performance.
I would like to send praise for enabling this experience with Oculus Go. The city of Shibuya and the nine-human model are shown statically, sometimes dynamically, and maintain performance. You will not feel VR sickness.

-Attractive character.
You can experience the story with a character designed by LAM, literally with a unique color. In addition, in the experience, you can taste the story with a sense of presence from the viewpoint of the character. I especially like Togoku Yuria.

I think the game will to carve the name in history of VR.
Thank you. Thank you very much.

reviews.review_title: A whole new way of experiencing a story
reviews.review_description: Tokyo Chronos is a visual novel in VR. I don't usually like reading texts in VR because it hinders immersion, but Tokyo Chronos did a great job of making reading in VR great fun. The way they show texts in VR is really cool, and they have cleverly combined the first person and the third person views to help you to understand the situation without having to rotate your head too much. The voice acting is also a top-notch, played by strong voice casts including Yūto Uemura (Hiro in Darling in the Franxx) and Yui Ishikawa (Violet in Violet Evergarden). A must experience.

reviews.review_title: It is dead. Who killed Battery?
reviews.review_description:
Even if the battery is dead.
Even if you feel sad inside.
You will surely come back.
Stand up again and again.
Please see until finale.
When the story comes to the end. 
What remains in your mind?

Thank you for the valuable experience.

My favorite VR game “東京クロノス”.
My favorite character “YURIA TOGOKU”.
百聞は一験にしかず。
